_meta.name: Training speed physical ceilings — ALM + CLM
_meta.description: Single source of truth for ALM/CLM training speed measurements, MFU, headroom, and remaining levers. Updated as new runs are measured.
_meta.session: 2026-04-17 — C4 F1-TC bf16 WMMA bench on RTX 3090 (hexa-c4 pod): 10.26x vs fp32 fused on canonical CLM 1B shape, correctness PASS (tol 1e-2)
_meta.status: in_progress — ALM r9 config locked (B1 consciousness loss + optimal batch), 2 H100 pods provisioned. CLM attn rewritten to hxblas_sgemm (26x, top-1 blocker resolved), nn_core 15 functions vectorized. Serving /hire/* + stratified30 + D-track consciousness pipeline live.

ALM.track: Qwen2.5-14B LoRA fine-tune on H100 (hexa launcher + embedded Python)

hardware.source_note: NVIDIA NeMo published Llama2-70B LoRA benchmarks, r7 measurement this session
hardware.pod_fleet_variance_note: Same software stack varies 31% step/min across pods (xhq9b2c8fljdyo vs ubzp169igb7tns). Runpod H100 SXM pods have heterogeneous performance. MFU measurements should tag pod_id.

r6.notes: Audit miscalibrated — real gain is from fused AdamW + TF32 + cudnn + pin_memory only

caveats: Not a clean Unsloth A/B — transformers 4.45→4.56, peft 0.13→0.18, torch 2.4→2.10+cu128 stack bump blended
Unsloth fast LoRA kernels actually DISABLED (48 layers patched, 0 kernels) due to lora_dropout=0.05
FA2 wheel broken on torch 2.10 → xformers fallback
r7.bonus: Loss descending faster at same step (5.80 @ step270 vs r6 ~10-11), phi_holo 4x at step200

batch_seq_sweep_2026_04_15.pod: xhq9b2c8fljdyo (r6 pod, freed after r6 finished 4000 steps)
key_findings: Micro-batch >> grad_accum — r7 b=1 g=8 was DEAD LAST. Free +8 MFU swapping g_accum→micro-batch.
seq scaling no benefit — seq=1024 wins on tok/s, longer seq loses MFU
VRAM sweet spot ~42 GB (b=8/s=1024), 38 GB headroom preserved
r7's 62.6% is unfair — optimal config same stack would measure ~71% directly

r8c_fp8_2026_04_15.verdict: FAIL — FP8 monkey-patch path broken on HF Qwen2 (random-init loss 15.2 + SLOWER than bf16 at 33.8% MFU)
r8c_fp8_2026_04_15.correctness_fail: te.Linear monkey-patch before from_pretrained causes ALL 48 layers' attn+MLP projections to load with RANDOM weights (HF warning: '_extra_state newly initialized' for every q/k/v/o/up/down/gate_proj). Loss=15 matches uniform prior over vocab 152k, not fine-tuning a pretrained model.
r8c_fp8_2026_04_15.speed_fail: -57.1% vs r7 (51.1→21.9 step/min), -47.2% vs r8a (41.5→21.9). FP8 overhead without compile/cudagraphs + grad ckpt 4x fwd recompute nullifies forward-only FP8 savings for LoRA.
r8c_fp8_2026_04_15.root_cause: Fundamental HF+TE integration gap. Patch disclaimer #1 warned about this. Correct path is accelerate.Accelerator(fp8_recipe_handler=...) OR full NeMo/Megatron native rewrite — neither in P4 scope.
r8c_fp8_2026_04_15.recommendation: ABANDON FP8 retrofit for current HF-based LoRA trainer. r8 ceiling push should pursue batch sweep #26, nn_core Tensor #27, or r8a Unsloth extensions #28.

r8a_stacked_2026_04_16.verdict: MEASURED on SLOW pod, fast-pod equivalent projects ~68-69% MFU — practical 75% ceiling NOT reached, FP8 (r8c) FAILED so r8a remains best r8 datapoint
r8a_stacked_2026_04_16.pod: xhq9b2c8fljdyo (slow, anima-p4-alm-holo) — fast pod ubzp169igb7tns TERMINATED after r7
r8a_stacked_2026_04_16.unsloth_fast_kernels: 96/144 layers patched (48 QKV + 48 O), 48 MLP still disabled (Qwen2 MLP bias term — Unsloth limit)

deltas.vs_unsloth_only_same_pod_clean_AB: +10.1% step/min, +1.6 MFU points (liger + lora_dropout=0 + 96-layer fast kernel unlock)
deltas.vs_r7_pod_variance_adjusted: +18.6% step/min relative (35.0 projected r7 on slow pod → r8a 41.5)
deltas.fast_pod_projection_mfu_pct: ~64-69% (depending on whether to use clean delta +1.6 or relative +18.6%)

next_levers: FP8 via TE — EXHAUSTED (r8c #29 FAILED — HF monkey-patch path broken, 33.8% MFU regression, loss=15 random-init)
Custom CUDA LoRA kernels (Unsloth-style for Qwen2 MLP bias term)
accelerate.Accelerator FP8 path (alternate FP8 integration, different from monkey-patch — NOT yet tried)
r8a_stacked_2026_04_16.agent_status: API 500 hit AFTER state file written, measurements complete and authoritative

stack_blocked: transformer_engine 2.13.0 — wheel 404 for torch 2.10+cu128, source build fails on missing cudnn.h

pod_fleet_variance.finding: SAME software stack measures 33.3 step/min on xhq9b2c8fljdyo vs 48.6 on ubzp169igb7tns at step 50 — 31% slower
pod_fleet_variance.implication: r7's 62.6% MFU partially pod-dependent. Ceiling varies by pod.

r8_partial_2026_04_15.unsloth_fast_lora_disabled_reason: lora_dropout=0.05 — log: 'patched 48 layers with 0 QKV/O/MLP layers'. Setting lora_dropout=0 unlocks +10-20%.
next_actions: r8a: --unsloth --liger + best_batch_from_#26_sweep, 150 steps on freed ubzp169igb7tns pod
r8b: lora_dropout=0 (unlock Unsloth fast LoRA kernels, +10-20% estimated)
r8c: fresh pod with torch==2.9 pin → transformer_engine install → --fp8 measurement (separate track)

clm_real_step_interpreter.config: d16, seq=8, 2-layer, flag ON (Track B cache)
clm_real_step_interpreter.notes: The 27.5x vs historical 27500ms phi-inline baseline is a legitimate speedup in phi compute but tiny vs physical ceiling

clm_phi_block_isolated_interpreter.source: bench_phi_cache_speedup.hexa, Track B cache lookup + anchor + contrastive (commit d8119005)

clm_phi_cache_loader_native_c.source: hexa native compile via 'hexa build phi_cache_loader.hexa -o ...' (commit 28f59cd8)
clm_phi_cache_loader_native_c.note: Best Mac-side MFU so far — but still 71x below 80% practical ceiling

clm_full_loop_native_microbench.honesty: start-dominated — exec overhead per iter, NOT steady-state; real MFU higher once amortized

clm_nn_core_tensor_ported_REAL_STEP.source: bench_train_step_tensor.hexa (commits worktree 36f49f44 + anima merge 15df1e1a + nexus state 640e7e8b)
clm_nn_core_tensor_ported_REAL_STEP.honest_caveat: at this shape (~50K flops/step) FFI-call-bound not FLOP-bound. 1479x is from eliminating list-append O(N²) churn, NOT BLAS throughput. Real 14B shapes (seq=512 d=4096+) expected to unlock additional 10-100x via BLAS
clm_nn_core_tensor_ported_REAL_STEP.train_clm_hexa_integration: deferred — 2170 LOC train_clm.hexa self-test cannot terminate on stage0 interpreter; needs P5a native interpreter rebuild
clm_nn_core_tensor_ported_REAL_STEP.mfu_pct: not yet computed at this shape — FFI-bound regime, MFU unstable

real_shape_sweep_2026_04_16.crossover_note: NO shape crossed the 30 GF/s BLAS-bound threshold. Peak 2.59 GF/s at d=768 seq=16 — 60.6x below Mac M3 156.7 GF/s ceiling, 48.5x below the 80% practical ceiling target.
real_shape_sweep_2026_04_16.diagnosis: The Tensor port successfully calls cblas_sgemm for matmul/matmul_transB/matmul_transA, but the surrounding scalar loops (rms_norm row-loop, softmax row-loop, swiglu element-loop, attention O(S²D) element-loop, cross-entropy row-loop, sgd element-loop, embed scatter loop) dominate total step time. `sample` on the live binary showed ~60% of CPU in layer_bwd_t → attn_bwd_t → hexa_mul/hexa_add boxed int arithmetic. Result: matmul share of total ms is tiny, so BLAS cannot become the bottleneck.
real_shape_sweep_2026_04_16.scaling_pattern: GF/s grows sub-linearly with D (0.54 @ d=16 → 2.59 @ d=768, ~4.8x over 48x D increase). With seq it saturates above 16 due to O(S²) attention scalar loop. Depth (nl) is neutral — extra layers scale ms linearly.
top_3_blockers: attn_fwd/attn_bwd inner loops O(S²D) scalar — should be 4 t_matmul calls
hexa codegen emits boxed int ops for index arithmetic in while bodies
real_shape_sweep_2026_04_16.next_action: Rewrite attn_fwd/attn_bwd using t_matmul for Q@K^T, probs@V, dctx@V^T — expected 10-50x at seq>=32; then emit native-int index arithmetic for additional 5-20x
real_shape_sweep_2026_04_16.honest_verdict: CLM nn_core Tensor port UNBLOCKS BLAS calls but does NOT yet engage BLAS-bound regime. The 1479x vs list-backed was real but occurred in FFI/alloc-overhead regime. Real BLAS throughput wins await attention rewrite + scalar-loop vectorisation. Best MFU 1.65% is 30x short of CLOSE (20%), 48x short of REACHED (80%).

CLM.diagnosis: BEFORE nn_core port: CLM was 3-4 orders of magnitude below physical ceiling on every path. AFTER nn_core Tensor port (#27 1479x) + hexa_v2 full-loop deploy (#18 251x): tiny shape 0.681 ms. AFTER real-shape sweep 2026-04-16 (18 shapes d=16..768 seq=8..32 nl=2..8): best MFU 1.65% at d=768 seq=16. 2026-04-16 evening: attention rewritten to hxblas_sgemm (26x at seq=128 d=256) + nn_core 6 functions vectorized via FFI (5-20x) + 9 new vectorized functions. Top-1 blocker (scalar attention loops) RESOLVED. MFU remeasurement pending to quantify new ceiling. Remaining: vectorise rmsnorm/softmax/swiglu remaining scalar paths, emit native-int index arithmetic in hexa codegen.

remaining_levers.name: Wire Tensor primitive into nn_core.hexa (matmul, linear, attention)
remaining_levers.status: DONE #27 → 1479x tiny shape, but sweep #30 2026-04-16 showed real-shape peak 1.65% MFU (d=768 seq=16). Matmul is BLAS but surrounding scalar loops dominate total ms.
remaining_levers.priority: NEXT LEVER = scalar-loop vectorisation + attention matmul rewrite

remaining_levers.name: Rewrite attn_fwd/attn_bwd to use t_matmul for Q@K^T + probs@V + gradients
remaining_levers.status: DONE 2026-04-16 — attention rewritten to hxblas_sgemm, 26x measured at seq=128 d=256. MFU remeasurement pending.

remaining_levers.name: Vectorise rms_norm / softmax / swiglu via vDSP (Accelerate) or cblas_sscal + cblas_snrm2
remaining_levers.expected_uplift: 5-20x on element-wise loops (currently 60-80% of ms at d>=128)
remaining_levers.status: PARTIAL 2026-04-16 — nn_core.hexa 6 functions vectorized via FFI (5-20x each), 9 new vectorized functions added. MFU remeasurement pending.
remaining_levers.priority: HIGH — partial progress, remaining scalar loops still need vectorization

remaining_levers.name: Emit native int arithmetic for index expressions in hexa codegen
remaining_levers.expected_uplift: 5-20x on inner scalar loops (boxed HexaVal unboxing)
remaining_levers.evidence: macOS sample on sweep #30 showed 60%+ CPU in hexa_mul/hexa_add runtime paths from attn_bwd_t index ops
remaining_levers.priority: HIGH — compounding multiplier on every inner loop

remaining_levers.name: C4 F1 fused QKV projection (training/c4_fused_kernels.hexa)
remaining_levers.expected_uplift: fp32 MVP: 1.75x at small shapes, 1.0x compute-bound. With F1-TC bf16 WMMA: ~1.4x launch + ~1.8x TC = ~2.5x at CLM 1B shapes per design doc §1
remaining_levers.status: fp32 MVP BENCH COMPLETE 2026-04-17 on RTX 3090 — numerical bit-exact (5 shapes), speedup 1.75x S1 small / 1.0x S3 design-doc. See shared/state/c4_f1_fused_qkv_bench_20260417.json. F1-TC bf16 WMMA path blocked on hexa-lang c2_struct_args ABI landing.
remaining_levers.priority: MEDIUM — unblocked by C2 landing; re-bench on H100 SXM once F1-TC lands

remaining_levers.name: C4 F1-TC bf16 WMMA fused QKV (training/c4_fused_kernels.hexa §1.5 + K_C4_QKV_PROJ_FUSED_TC_FWD)
remaining_levers.expected_uplift_design_doc: 2.5x cumulative vs unfused baseline (1.4x launch + 1.8x TC)
remaining_levers.measured_uplift_rtx_3090: 10.26x vs fp32 fused / 10.40x vs unfused on S3 canonical CLM 1B (M=4096 K_in=1024 D_head=64 H=16) — 4.16x above design doc forecast. S1 small 2.14x, S2 medium 9.44x, S5 large_head 9.90x. TFLOPS: fp32 0.60 → TC 6.17 on RTX 3090 (3090 TC peak ~140 = 4.4% of peak single-kernel).
remaining_levers.correctness: PASS — TC rel 2.08e-3 on canonical shape (tol 1e-2, 4.8x margin). Max rel across 5 shapes = 2.08e-3. fp32 path bit-exact.
remaining_levers.status: F1-TC BF16 WMMA BENCH COMPLETE 2026-04-17 on RTX 3090. c2_struct_args ABI blocker cleared 2026-04-16 (hexa-lang 3760238). See shared/state/c4_f1_tc_bf16_bench_20260417.json. Hexa impl: c4_set_tc_mode(1) toggles dispatcher. C2 ABI not actually needed — NVRTC arg-pointer array path used.
remaining_levers.priority: HIGH — H100 SXM MFU measurement next (bench pod = 3090, absolute TFLOPS need H100 for gate §1.2 55% MFU)
remaining_levers.gate_verdict: CORRECTNESS + COMPONENT_SPEEDUP PASS — design doc 2.5x gate exceeded 4.16x. MFU gate deferred to H100 SXM re-bench + cross-layer fusion composition.
next_lever_chain: H100 SXM re-bench (30 min, $1.5) → absolute TFLOPS number + MFU % of 989 TFLOPS peak
cudagraph capture (60 min, free) → small-shape launch-overhead elimination
F1-TC backward (180 min) → mirror fwd for gradient, 2.0x forecast
F2 lmhead TC + F3 FFN swiglu TC (240+360 min) → design doc §3 2nd+3rd priority

update_protocol.rule: Any training run that produces a new MFU measurement MUST update this file before closing the session
update_protocol.owner: Claude Code + training agents (autonomous update allowed)
update_protocol.ceiling_recheck_trigger: Every new major run (r8, r9, ... / CLM native deploy, Tensor port, etc.)
